_comment: Keywords match against x-mitre-data-component names (lowercase) from ATT&CK STIX v10+. Format: exact substring of the component name. Add new entries without touching app.js.

_meta.source: MITRE ATT&CK STIX v10+, Suricata, Sysmon, Wazuh, Windows Event Log

categories.label: Network / Suricata
categories.capability: Network traffic inspection and signature-based detection at the packet/flow level
categories.notes: Requires inline or TAP/SPAN deployment. Best for detecting network IOCs, C2, exfil, and lateral movement over the wire.

categories.label: Process / Sysmon
categories.capability: Deep process-level visibility: creation, access, injection, network connections, and file/registry events generated by processes
categories.notes: Requires a tuned Sysmon config (e.g., SwiftOnSecurity). Event ID mapping: 1=ProcessCreate, 3=NetworkConnect, 7=ImageLoad, 8=CreateRemoteThread, 10=ProcessAccess, 11=FileCreate, 13=RegSetValue, 22=DnsQuery.

categories.label: Host / Wazuh
categories.capability: Host-based telemetry: file integrity, registry changes, service management, scheduled tasks, kernel/driver activity, and cloud workload events
categories.notes: Cross-platform agent collects FIM, log analysis, SCA, and vulnerability data. Integrates with MITRE ATT&CK mapping natively since Wazuh 4.x.

categories.label: Auth / Windows Event
categories.capability: Authentication and identity telemetry: logon events, AD object changes, user account lifecycle, and credential operations
categories.notes: Key Event IDs: 4624 (logon), 4625 (failed logon), 4672 (special privilege), 4720 (account created), 4732 (group member added), 4740 (lockout), 1102 (audit log cleared). Requires Advanced Audit Policy configuration.

categories.label: Cloud / API Logs
categories.capability: Cloud-native API and control plane telemetry: resource provisioning, storage access, identity operations, and container/Kubernetes events
categories.notes: AWS CloudTrail covers API calls. Azure Monitor covers resource events. GCP Cloud Logging covers audit logs. Kubernetes audit logs required for cluster-level detection.
